selection_rule: include only layer3 rows where ready_for_v1 == yes

status_model.notes: Layer 4 freezes only the first MVP field inventory and first MVP rule pack. Future runtime objects should track both the field value and an evidence status instead of forcing every field into a binary yes/no state.

fields.description: Explanation text, script, or material bundle used to communicate key product terms and risks to the consumer.
Status or role of an intermediary, agent, or broker relevant to what conduct or disclosure rules apply.
Core product terms that must be disclosed so the consumer can understand what is being offered.
Detected prohibited claim, misleading claim, or other disallowed promotional signal in visible content.
Required deposit or savings disclaimer, including guarantee or protection limitations where applicable.
Required investment-related risk or caution disclosure that must be surfaced to the consumer.
Loan-related cost, fee, or penalty disclosures that must be visible to the consumer.
Information showing when interest is charged or how the timing of interest accrual is communicated.
Interest rate range, rate basis, or rate calculation method disclosed for a loan product.
Visible identification of the product itself in customer-facing content or documents.
Visible identification of the financial seller or responsible institution in customer-facing content.
Evidence that advisory conduct preserves required independence, neutrality, or conflict controls.
Evidence that the required explanation was confirmed, acknowledged, or otherwise completed.
Required insurance-related warning, replacement notice, or caution text that must be surfaced to the consumer.
Loan conditions that must be disclosed to explain the structure of the loan offer.
Activity log or archived record showing that a regulated action, explanation, or solicitation occurred.
Evidence that required contract or pre-contract documents were delivered or made available.
Internal control standard or formal compliance procedure document required by the organization.
Evidence that records are preserved with integrity, retention, and access safeguards.
Structured adequacy assessment result or completion state for products or consumers that require adequacy review.
Collected consumer profile facts used to judge suitability, adequacy, or tailored explanation duties.
Registry or record set tracking staff, intermediaries, or other regulated personnel information.
Record of a consumer request to access, inspect, or receive regulated information or records.
Record of the organization's response to a regulated consumer request or access event.
Consumer classification needed for suitability or adequacy handling, such as general or professional consumer status.
Reviewer-facing fairness or consumer-protection guardrail used when a rule is still partially principle-driven.
Identity of the representative, staff member, or solicitor involved in the consumer interaction.
Purpose statement or context showing why a solicitation contact or interaction is being made.
Structured suitability assessment result or completion state derived from the consumer profile and product context.
